text: The conceptual-bridge NEGATIVE result is NOT a sampling artifact -- closing the methodological caveat repeated verbatim across a-0036..a-0041 ('same top-30-cluster/top-30-longest-proof high-similarity sample'). Every probe that concluded 'no in-corpus representation recovers Brouwer<->Lawvere-style conceptual bridges' ran on the edit-distance detector's biased selection (largest clusters x LONGEST proofs), so the corpus-limit conclusion (a-0041) could in principle have been an artifact of looking only at long proofs from big domains. e-0046 re-runs the core a-0036 genericity test (do the 200 MIN-closest cross-cluster proof pairs share domain-specific <=3-cluster tactic tokens, or only ubiquitous scaffolding?) under five proof-selection regimes -- longest (=a-0036 baseline), median-length, SHORTEST, and RANDOM proofs, plus a smaller-cluster-half variant -- holding token genericity GLOBAL so only the selection changes. The repro regime reproduces a-0036 exactly (ED range 0.586-0.833, matching a-0009's 'closest ED 0.586'; 2/200 specific; generic_frac 0.44). Across ALL five regimes the fraction of bridge pairs sharing >=1 domain-specific token is <=0.02 (<=4/200), never above the random cross-cluster control floor (<=0.0003), and the shared-token mass stays generic-scaffolding-dominated (bridges 0.36-0.67 generic, comparable to control 0.40-0.51). short_proofs even surfaces IDENTICAL (ED=0.0) cross-cluster proofs, but they are pure generic scaffolding. So the result is invariant to which proofs and which clusters the detector looks at: structurally-similar cross-domain proofs share generic method scaffolding, never domain-specific shared structure, regardless of sample. This hardens a-0041 -- the operational/conceptual bridge gap (a-0009/a-0001) is a genuine corpus/representation limit, not a tunable-feature OR sampling choice. The one remaining escape hatch is unchanged: typed-statement structure or an external NL embedding, which is OUT of the LeanDojo declaration corpus.
